title: Linear Models, Regression, and Systems · Spiral Review
summary: Allocate staff and supplies under multiple linear constraints. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a linear allocation and scenario model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Fit and interpret linear rate and intercept parameters with units and domain limits.
Fit a linear regression and inspect residuals before using its predictions.
Solve linear systems and classify unique, absent, and infinite solutions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

stimulus.title: Linear Decision case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: math intermediate linear systems. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: evidence

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Linear Decision applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use sensitivity and shadow interpretations to defend a constrained linear decision.
